title: What would you do with 10 hours free consultancy with a monday.com expert?
excerpt:

posts.author: Robbie-Omnitas (Robbie Perris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Today might be your lucky day!

We need your help to shape a research article on the most common workflow challenges and how to solve them.

Can you spare 60 seconds to answer this question?

What was your team’s biggest workflow challenge of 2025?

In return for your support we are offering one lucky business that chance to win 10 HOURS of custom mentoring and training with our expert monday.com consultants.

So for your chance to win follow the link below

https://lnkd.in/ea6mi-A9

Submissions close: Feb 28
Winner announced in early March

posts.author: lAMhUAN21 (hUAN)
posts.cookedHtml: For us, the biggest challenge has been over-automation. We built so many automations that it actually became harder to track what was triggering what. We’d love guidance on structuring scalable workflows without creating complexity.

posts.author: sophiasmith (Sophia Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d use the time to clean up the core workflow first like making one solid intake to delivery board with the right automations and permissions that’s where most teams lose hours. Then I’d ask them to review a real project in my account and show me what to simplify so the system stays easy to use after the sessions end.

posts.author: erica.damsten (Erica Damsten)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a great opportunity to get the help you need if you’ve been struggling with something in monday.com lately!

posts.author: Julia.Palm.Omnitas (Julia Palm)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m biased, but those 10 hours of mentoring are gold! Having seen the challenges our clients faced last year, I’m really curious to see if the survey results mirror what we saw on the front lines. Nice work putting this together!

posts.author: Robbie-Omnitas (Robbie Perris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Sophia,

Thank you for your reply!

Please can I ask that you complete the monday form below to entered for a chance to win.

forms.monday.com

posts.author: gpenpraze (Gareth Penpraze)
posts.cookedHtml: Having worked with Omnitas 1st hand on some workflows, these 10 hours are invaluable!! I think I’ll stick a cheeky submission in too Robbie.
